english: če* (che) W.; baṅ gráṅ-pa (bang grang pa) to run a race; baṅ-rtsál sbyóṅ-ba (bang rtsal sbyong ba) Mil. to exercise one's self in racing; baṅ daṅ _gró-las-dag-gis nyén-pa (bang dang 'gro las dag gis nyen pa) or baṅ-_grós nyén-pa (bang 'gros nyen pa) to overexert one's self in running Med.; *bhaṅ-gyóg (bang gyog), bhaṅ-čóṅ* (bang chong) C. running-match, race; baṅ-čén (bang chen) (-pa, also -po) Pth., Glr. swift messenger, courier; *bhaṅ-mi* (bang mi) C., *baṅ-mi* (bang mi) W., id. - 2. v. baṅ-ba (bang ba).

store-room, store-house, corn magazine, also treasury Dzl.; šiṅ-baṅ (shing bang) Kun. a large box for grain, half underground; baṅ-pud (bang phud) first-fruit offering from the barn; *bhaṅ-gha* (bang gha) Ts. repository; (dbus-baṅ (dbus bang), pronounced:) *u-bháṅ* (u bhang) Ts. cupboard, press, case.
= kri-_páṅ (khri 'phang), the part of the mčód-rten (mchod rten) which has the form of a staircase. — 2. Sch. 'a separate part of a house connected by a staircase' (?).

grave, tomb, ysón-por báṅ-sor _dzúg-pa (gson por bang sor 'dzug pa) to bury alive Glr.; sepulchre, monument, báṅ-so _débs-pa (bang so 'debs pa), or rtsíg-pa (rtsig pa) to build a sepulchre Glr.; báṅ-so mčód-pa (bang so mchod pa) to perform funeral sacrifices, to honour a grave Glr.
Sch. 1. = sbáṅs-pa (sbangs pa). — 2. = báṅ-ba (bang ba). — 3. = báṅ-so (bang so).
(Hindi) 1. a weight = 2 ser, about 4 pounds. — 2. balance, pair of scales; *bát-ti tág-če* (bad ti tag ce) to weigh W.

1. moisture, humidity, *šiṅ bad k'ór-na* (shing bad khor na) W. when wood attracts humidity; *bád-čan* (bad can) moist, humid, damp, from rain or dew W. — 2. hoar-frost bá-mo (ba mo) Sch., Wts. — 3. in compounds for bád-kan (bad kan). — 4. edge, border, bad ni yser (bad ni gser) the edge is of gold Sch.; mk'ar-bád (mkhar bad) S.g. = k'a-bad? (kha bad) bud-búr (bud bur) Mil.?
C. a plant, similar to mustard, yielding oil.

mucus phlegm, a. as normal substance of the body comprizing 5 kinds: rten-byéd (rten byed) mucus in the joints of the neck and shoulders, myag-byéd (myag byed) in the stomach, myoṅ-byéd (myong byed) in the tongue and palate, tsim-byéd (tshim byed) in the brain, eyes etc., _byor-byéd ('byor byed) in the rest of the joints; b. in a morbid state, as a cause of disease: bád-kan-las gyúr-pai nad (bad kan las gyur pa'i nad) mucous diseases; bad-kan-lhén (bad kan lhen) mucus in the cardiac regions, prob. = gastric catarrh; bad-kan-lčags-drégs (bad kan lcags dregs) intestinal catarrh; bad-kan-mgul-gágs (bad kan mgul gags) mucous consumption; bád-kan grám-bu dkár-po (bad kan gram bu dkar po) etc. Med.; bad-kan-rlúṅ (bad kan rlung) phlegm and air, bad-kan-mk'rís (bad kan mkhris) phlegm and bile; bad-kan-krag (bad kan khrag) phlegm and blood Med.
1. C. beer-jug, pitcher. — 2. v. the following articles.

a little, a bit; kyod-ráṅ nyams-bun-bún-gyi snáṅ-ba-la (khyod rang nyams bun bun gyi snang ba la) you, with your little bit of spiritual light Mil.; rtsi-šiṅ sna-tsógs ban-ma-bún (rtsi shing sna tshogs ban ma bun) forest-trees of every kind not a few (or also variously mixed?) Mil.; ban-če (ban che) in moderate quantity, 'tolerably many'.
Sch. skull, cranium; frq. spelled bhán-dha (bhan dha), hence perh. = भाण्ड vessel, in which sense it is gen. to be understood in books; accordingly it may be a skull used as a drinking-vessel.

acc. to Hodgson's learned Nepalese authority (Illustr. 75) = बन्द्य, reverendus, salutandus, for which also in the Tibetan language btsún-pa (btsun pa) is always used as an equivalent: a Buddhist priest; hence originally = Buddhist in general, the term being also applied to women Mil.; ban-rgán (ban rgan) an old priest Glr.; ban-spráṅ (ban sprang) and spraṅ-bán (sprang ban) a mendicant friar; ban-čúṅ (ban chung) ('pen-kiong' Desg. 370) pupil, disciple in a monastery; ban-lóg (ban log) col., a priest that has turned apostate; ban-bón (ban bon), Mil. and elsewh. 1. (acc. to our Lama:) Buddhist and Bonpo. 2. (acc. to Sch): a Bon-priest, in which case, however, the word prob would be bon-bán (bon ban).
Sch.: for bag-zón (bag zon) dread, fear.
hastiness, rashness, want of consideration in speaking and acting = yzu-lúm (gzu lum); sdig-pa bab-čól-du byéd-pa (sdig pa bab col du byed pa) to sin recklessly, without heed or regard Mil.
